headline: CATTLE COMPLEX SPLITS WIDER AS WEEK ENDS
subheadline: Live cattle breaks $242 floor while feeders hold $352, resolving week's convergence question through weakness.
lead: The cattle complex answered this week's question the hard way: live cattle broke $242 support to close at $241.15, down half a percent, while feeders held $352.88 on the same decline. The 4.6% spread between live and feeders that looked ready to compress Monday is now widening again. Five days of watching for convergence through strength got convergence through synchronized weakness instead.
the_takeaway: Cattle convergence came through weakness, not strength, as live broke key support.
teaser: Live cattle breaks $242 support while feeders hold, answering the week's convergence question through weakness.

one_number.unit: oil's May decline on Iran ceasefire hopes
one_number.context: WTI crude is set for its worst month in over a year as geopolitical premium unwinds faster than it built up. When energy crashes this hard, it pulls input costs lower but also signals broader demand concerns that ripple into protein markets.

yesterdays_call.summary: Called geopolitical premium unwinding faster than it built up as crude cratered on Iran ease.
yesterdays_call.note: Oil's 19% May decline confirms the premium is draining exactly as forecast.

sections.title: Cattle Break Support Together
sections.body: Live cattle closed $241.15, breaking the $242 floor that held for three sessions and settling at the lowest close in two weeks. Feeders dropped the same half-percent to $352.88 but stayed above their $350 technical level. **The convergence trade died through weakness, not strength.** Boxed beef was sharply lower during the session, pulling cash values with it. The weekly cattle on feed report due Tuesday will reset the fundamental story, but the chart damage is already done.
sections.catalyst: Boxed beef values sharply lower, pulling cash cattle with futures
sections.bottom_line: Support broken, convergence through weakness, Tuesday's report resets the story.
sections.vs_yesterday: Cattle: yesterday's Iran ease thesis played out, oil's 19% May drop confirmed.

sections.title: Grains Drift in Memorial Lull
sections.body: Corn closed $4.5275, down 3 cents in the thinnest trade of the week. Soybeans eased a penny to $11.98. Chicago wheat managed a quarter-cent gain to $6.23, the only green on the board. **No catalyst worth naming, just Memorial Day weekend position-squaring.** The eastern Belt weather forecast shows cooler, drier conditions next week, but planting pace is already ahead of normal and the prevented plant deadline is still a week away. Range-bound consolidation, not direction.
sections.bottom_line: Holiday drift, no weather premium yet, prevented plant deadline still a week out.

outside_the_pit.title: FTC launches fertilizer antitrust investigation
outside_the_pit.body: The Federal Trade Commission confirmed it's investigating possible anti-competitive practices in the U.S. fertilizer industry. Rising input costs that weren't fully explained by supply chain disruptions are now under federal scrutiny.

outside_the_pit.title: USDA allows base acre updates for farm programs
outside_the_pit.body: Landowners can now adjust base acres, potentially increasing farm program support. The timing matters with prevent plant season approaching and crop insurance decisions still on the table.

outside_the_pit.title: Tyson settles beef price-fixing case for $47 million
outside_the_pit.body: The settlement covers commercial beef purchasers, separate from the ongoing producer class action. Another sign the packer concentration story isn't going away despite recent cattle rallies.

spread_to_watch.label: Live cattle / feeder ratio
spread_to_watch.commentary: Monday this ratio looked ready to compress as both contracts gained together. Instead it's widening as live cattle breaks support while feeders hold their floor. The cattle complex is splitting again, not converging.

basis.headline: Eastern Belt corn basis steady despite futures weakness
basis.body: **Local corn basis holding firm** as Memorial Day weekend thins trade but doesn't kill demand. Ethanol plants stayed online through the holiday and river logistics keep moving old-crop. Western Belt staying softer, consistent with the seasonal. The basis bid is telling you the cash market isn't following futures lower yet.

weekly_thread.question: Will the cattle complex split resolve through feeder strength or live cattle weakness?
weekly_thread.status_text: Live cattle weakness won. The $242 support break while feeders held $350 answered the week's convergence question through synchronized decline, not the upward resolution the chart wanted.

the_more_you_know.title: The Memorial Day fade: why holiday Fridays drain conviction
the_more_you_know.body: Corn down 3 cents on the year's thinnest trade tells the Memorial Day story: nobody wants to hold risk into a three-day weekend. Holiday Fridays consistently show position-squaring rather than new conviction, even when fundamentals argue for moves. The pros know Monday's first hour will reset whatever Friday's thin trade decided. Today's price action was about calendar, not catalysts.

watch_list.desc: Weekly Cattle on Feed report; above 102% of last year keeps pressure on live cattle
USDA Crop Progress; corn above 85% planted removes weather premium entirely
Weekly petroleum inventories; builds above 5M barrels extend oil's slide

daily_quote.text: Learn from yesterday, live for today, hope for tomorrow.

source_summary: CME, CBOT, NYMEX closes; USDA, FTC, industry reports
